subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Acute vs Chronic Poisoning
description: A short lesson to help you apply Acute vs Chronic Poisoning.

hook.text: A single dose of cyanide kills in minutes. A lifetime of lead exposure destroys slowly.

buildup.text: Acute poisoning is a sudden, high-dose exposure with immediate symptoms.

discovery.text: Chronic poisoning accumulates over time—small doses building up until damage appears.

twist.text: Lead paint in old homes causes chronic poisoning in children—lowering IQ without obvious symptoms.

climax.text: Chronic poisoning is harder to detect and often misdiagnosed as other conditions.

punchline.text: The loudest poisons kill fast. The quietest ones take years.

quiz.question: What makes chronic poisoning particularly dangerous?
options: It accumulates slowly and is hard to detect
